name: Concourse
description: AI agent platform for corporate finance teams that connects to ERPs, data warehouses, billing, payroll, and CRM systems and automates financial analysis, reporting, and natural-language data queries with source-traceable outputs.

disruptionMechanisms: Automates manual financial analysis and reporting
Reduces reliance on spreadsheet-based workflows
Natural-language interface over structured financial systems
attributes.businessModel: Subscription SaaS with free entry tier
attributes.problemStatement: Corporate finance teams spend excessive manual effort on financial analysis, reporting, and reconciling data across disconnected ERP, warehouse, and billing systems.
attributes.productDescription: Concourse is an AI agent platform that connects to a company's financial systems (ERPs, data warehouses, billing, payroll, CRM) and lets finance teams query data in natural language, generate CFO-ready reports on a schedule, and export results with every number traceable to source data.

founders.name: Matthieu Hafemeister
founders.background: Name appears in an AlleyWatch article URL slug covering Concourse's Series A; not confirmed as founder on Concourse's own site or financing press release. Confirm in diligence.

name: Dust
description: Paris-based 'multiplayer' agentic AI platform that lets employees and AI agents share workspaces and context across an enterprise, connecting to internal tools like Notion, Google Drive, Slack, and Intercom.

disruptionMechanisms: Shifts enterprise AI from single-player assistants to shared multiplayer agent workflows
Displaces point AI-assistant tools with a workspace-level control plane
attributes.problemStatement: Enterprise AI adoption has been limited to isolated single-user assistants rather than collaborative, auditable workflows shared across teams and agents.
attributes.productDescription: Dust is a multiplayer agentic AI platform connecting humans and AI agents in shared enterprise workspaces, with deep integrations into internal knowledge and communication tools, reporting 3,000+ organizations, 51,000 monthly active users, and zero churn in 2025.

founders.name: Stanislas Polu
founders.background: OpenAI alumnus; co-founded Dust to build enterprise multiplayer AI assistants from Paris.

founders.name: Gabriel Hubert
founders.background: Co-founded Dust alongside Polu; named in TechCrunch's 2024 Series A coverage.

name: Squint
description: Mobile AI/AR platform that captures expert factory-operator knowledge from video and auto-generates step-by-step guided procedures for manufacturing workers, with customers including PepsiCo, Michelin, and Ford.

disruptionMechanisms: Replaces manual/paper SOPs with AI-generated, video-derived procedures
Digitizes tacit tribal knowledge held by experienced operators
attributes.problemStatement: Manufacturers lose critical operational knowledge as experienced operators retire or turn over, and onboarding new workers to complex procedures is slow and error-prone.
attributes.productDescription: Squint uses multimodal generative AI to turn a video recording of an expert operator performing a task into interactive AR navigation, multimedia tutorials, forms, and digital notes, cutting new-operator time-to-value by over 50% per company claims.

founders.name: Devin Bhushan
founders.background: Previously built features for Yahoo's fantasy-sports platform, where he connected with investors Tim Tully and Jess Lee, who later backed Squint's Series A and B.

name: Kick
description: AI-native, self-driving bookkeeping platform for small businesses, solopreneurs, and accounting firms, automating transaction categorization, receipt matching, and financial reporting.

disruptionMechanisms: Automates manual SMB bookkeeping historically done by human bookkeepers
Reduces cost of outsourced bookkeeping via AI automation
attributes.problemStatement: Small businesses and solopreneurs face expensive, error-prone, and slow manual bookkeeping, and prior automated solutions (e.g., Bench) have failed to sustain the category.
attributes.productDescription: Kick auto-categorizes transactions, matches receipts, and produces profit-and-loss and balance-sheet reporting for SMBs and accounting firms, integrating with Plaid, Stripe, PayPal, Mercury, Ramp, and Gusto, serving 3,000+ businesses.

founders.name: Conrad Wadowski
founders.background: Publishes Kick's funding announcements under the Kick Finance Medium publication; identified as founder in Axios's October 2024 seed coverage.

round: Series A (public sources describe the Nov. 2025 round simply as new funding, not explicitly labeled Series A)

name: Artisan
description: Builder of 'AI employees' starting with Ava, an autonomous AI Business Development Representative that prospects, personalizes outreach, and books meetings for sales teams.

disruptionMechanisms: Automates SDR prospecting and outbound historically done by human sales development reps
Consolidates prospecting, research, and outreach into one agent
attributes.goToMarket: Enterprise and mid-market sales, PLG trial
attributes.problemStatement: Outbound sales development is labor-intensive, expensive to scale with human SDRs, and inconsistent in personalization and follow-up.
attributes.productDescription: Artisan's Ava is an AI Business Development Rep that discovers leads, researches buying signals (job changes, funding, leadership moves), writes personalized multi-channel outreach, and books meetings autonomously; the company is building additional agents Aaron (inbound) and Aria (scheduling).

founders.name: Jaspar Carmichael-Jack
founders.background: Named in TechCrunch's April 2025 Series A coverage as CEO and public spokesperson; additional co-founders not identified in sources reviewed.

name: Numeric
description: AI-powered close-automation platform unifying close management, cash reconciliation, and reporting for enterprise and mid-market accounting teams, used by Brex, Plaid, Wealthfront, and OpenAI.

technicalApproaches: AI transaction matching and anomaly detection
disruptionMechanisms: Automates manual month-end close and reconciliation work
Displaces spreadsheet-based close processes
attributes.problemStatement: Enterprise accounting teams spend days on manual month-end close, reconciliation, and variance analysis using disconnected spreadsheets and legacy tools.
attributes.productDescription: Numeric automates close checklists, account reconciliation, and cash matching (90%+ automated), with AI-generated variance explanations and CFO-ready reporting, expanding from close management toward a broader finance-data platform.

founders.name: Parker Gilbert
founders.background: Prior VP of Finance experience at Hearth, per Numeric's own Series B announcement.

founders.name: Anthony Alvernaz
founders.background: Named in TechCrunch's October 2024 Series A coverage; specific background not detailed in sources reviewed.

founders.name: Andrew Bihl
founders.background: Named in TechCrunch's October 2024 Series A coverage; specific background not detailed in sources reviewed.

name: HappyRobot
description: Agentic AI platform deploying autonomous 'AI workers' to automate phone-based and multi-channel operational communication, initially for freight logistics and now expanding across industries.

targetCustomers: Enterprises across utilities, airlines, retail, manufacturing, insurance, financial services
disruptionMechanisms: Automates manual phone-based freight coordination and communication
Displaces human call-center labor for repetitive operational calls
regulatoryLabels: SOC 2 / GDPR / HIPAA / EU AI Act / NIST CSF / DORA compliant
attributes.problemStatement: Freight brokerage and logistics operations depend on high-volume, repetitive phone communication (carrier sales, check calls, POD collection) that is expensive and hard to scale with human staff.
attributes.productDescription: HappyRobot deploys AI voice and multi-channel agents that handle inbound/outbound carrier communication, check calls, and document collection, with a governance layer for behavioral evaluation and auditing, serving 150+ enterprises including DHL, Schneider, and Uber Freight.

founders.name: Pablo
founders.background: AI research background; full surname not disclosed in HappyRobot's own Series A announcement.

founders.name: Luis
founders.background: AI research background; full surname not disclosed in HappyRobot's own Series A announcement.

founders.name: Javi
founders.background: Shipper-side logistics operating experience; full surname not disclosed in HappyRobot's own Series A announcement.

name: Spellbook
description: AI copilot for transactional lawyers, integrated into Microsoft Word, offering contract review, drafting, benchmarking, and playbook-driven workflows for law firms and in-house legal teams.

technicalApproaches: LLM-based drafting and review (GPT-5 and others)
disruptionMechanisms: Accelerates contract drafting and review historically performed manually by associates
Encodes firm-specific standards into reusable AI playbooks
attributes.problemStatement: Transactional lawyers spend excessive hours drafting, reviewing, and benchmarking contracts using manual, Word-based workflows.
attributes.productDescription: Spellbook integrates into Microsoft Word to review and redline contracts, draft from templates, benchmark against industry standards, answer legal questions, encode firm playbooks, and run multi-document agentic workflows via its 'Associate' feature, serving 4,500+ legal teams across 80+ countries.

founders.name: Scott Stevenson
founders.background: Identified as co-founder and CEO in SiliconANGLE's October 2025 Series B coverage; publicly frames Spellbook's positioning as legal work's 'spreadsheet moment.'

name: Basis
description: AI agent platform that automates structured accounting workflows (tax, audit, reconciliation, document review) end-to-end for professional accounting firms and in-house accounting departments.

targetCustomers: Accounting firms (client accounting services, tax, audit)
technicalApproaches: Autonomous LLM agents with rules-based controls
disruptionMechanisms: Automates structured accounting labor across tax, audit, and advisory workflows
Addresses capacity constraints in the accounting profession
attributes.problemStatement: Accounting firms face a growing capacity crisis, with too few qualified accountants to handle tax, audit, and advisory workloads using traditional manual processes.
attributes.productDescription: Basis builds autonomous agents that perform accounting work end-to-end across tax preparation, audit testing, reconciliation, and document review, reporting that roughly 30% of the Top 25 U.S. accounting firms use its agents, with named customers including Boulay PLLP, Clark Nuber PS, MarksNelson LLC, Pinion LLC, and UHY LLC.

founders.name: Matt Harpe
founders.background: Prior Boston Consulting Group and SoftBank executive experience, per SiliconANGLE's February 2026 Series B coverage.

founders.name: Zenna Tavares
founders.background: Innovation scholar at Columbia University's Zuckerman Mind Brain Behavior Institute; identified as a founding researcher by an industry newsletter (nextplayso.substack.com), not confirmed on Basis's own site in materials reviewed.

founders.name: Emily Mackevicius
founders.background: Postdoctoral researcher studying memory in birds at the Aronov lab and Center for Theoretical Neuroscience; identified as a founding researcher by the same secondary source.

founders.name: Eli Bingham
founders.background: Machine learning fellow at the Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard, co-creator of the Pyro probabilistic-programming language, and former senior research scientist at Uber AI Labs; identified as a founding researcher by the same secondary source.

name: Eve
description: AI platform purpose-built for plaintiff law firms, automating case intake, medical chronologies, drafting, and discovery to help personal-injury and labor/employment firms handle more cases with the same headcount.

disruptionMechanisms: Automates document- and data-heavy plaintiff case preparation
Increases case throughput per attorney at plaintiff firms
attributes.goToMarket: Enterprise sales into plaintiff law firms
attributes.problemStatement: Plaintiff law firms are chronically under-resourced relative to case volume, spending excessive attorney and staff time on case intake, medical chronology preparation, drafting, and discovery.
attributes.productDescription: Eve's EveOS platform automates case intake and evaluation, medical chronologies and overviews, legal document and demand-letter drafting, and discovery management for plaintiff law firms, reporting 100+ firms live and 500% year-over-year revenue growth at its Series A.

founders.name: Jay Madheswaran
founders.background: 15+ years in AI/ML at Facebook and Rubrik (first engineer, head of product engineering); previously at Lightspeed Venture Partners, which later co-led Eve's Series A.

founders.name: Matt Noe
founders.background: Founding engineer at Rubrik with roughly a decade of AI product-development experience.

founders.name: David Zeng
founders.role: Co-founder and Chief Architect / Head of Engineering
founders.background: 10+ years of AI and machine-learning experience, per Eve's company page.
